title: Bouldering, Broken Phones and (B)hot Springs
summary: After leaving Bozeman we were aiming to check out a campground on a lake but after a half mile into an 8 mile dirt road we decided to bail because the washboards were rattling everything in our van and it sounded like it was coming apart at the seams. We ended up finding a decent spot at a fishing access site where we spent the afternoon reading, me working on my embroidery and Maya keeping a close eye on all the groundhogs surrounding our van. The next day was supposed to be another cold, rainy day so we headed to check out Yellowstone Hot Springs. We spent a few hours soaking in the hot tub and warm pool. Colin went on a run down the road (I also tried to walk but bailed because it was so windy). We found a new camp that night, a nice little spot right off of the road which happened to have some good bouldering. So although we have no bouldering pads, we walked around with our shoes on trying a couple of easy ones. During this escapade, Colin left his phone on the ground and he happened to land on it when he jumped off the boulder and it broke entirely (so that was unfortunate).
